rawCommits.subject: feat(analytics): add session_active event for accurate DAU tracking
rawCommits.cleanSubject: add session_active event for accurate DAU tracking

rawCommits.subject: fix(analytics): rename purchase/subscription events to reflect checkout intent, not completion
rawCommits.cleanSubject: rename purchase/subscription events to reflect checkout intent, not completion

rawCommits.subject: revert: restore shell chrome, mode selector pill, and shared right-click menu
rawCommits.body:
Reverts 2a5a27ee8 ("refactor(interface): trim shell chrome and input UI
experiments"), which swept away polished UI work in favor of a thinner
zui-Topbar / plain mode-pill / inline right-click setup. That sweep
caused multiple visible regressions:

- Mode selector lost its measured/animated indicator pill and the
  min-height matching --control-height-sm, so the row no longer reads
  as the same height as the input control.
- Desktop titlebar dropped the bespoke ShellTitlebar + MenuBar +
  compact UpdatePill, falling back to a plain Topbar with the floating
  UpdateBanner re-attached as a sibling. Update affordance lost its
  compact pill placement next to the theme toggle.
- BottomTaskbar stopped wiring useDesktopContextMenu, so right-clicks
  on the empty taskbar area no longer opened the desktop context menu.
- DesktopApp re-implemented its own inline portal, losing both the
  shared menu's viewport-aware bottom/right anchoring (so the menu
  flew off-screen near the taskbar) and its Settings item.
- AgentEditorForm stacked Name/Role, renamed "Advanced" to "Advanced
  options", and added an extra Advanced header above the runtime
  section.
- InputBarShell removed the inputRowEnd slot and the inline
  CommandChips variant, so slash command tags no longer render inline
  next to the textarea.
- 125 lines of right-click context menu test coverage in
  BottomTaskbar.test.tsx were dropped along with the wiring.

Restoring 2a5a27ee8 brings back all of the above. The two conflicts
introduced by post-trim commits (42ba4dc69's mode-selector tint and
ae662311f's textarea focus comment) are resolved by taking the
pre-trim shape; the per-mode tint is re-ported in the follow-up
commit so the per-mode accent lands on the restored indicator pill
instead of the now-removed plain segment background.

Mixpanel analytics, the onboarding stack, and the Settings page
Updates row layout fixes (0ad443224, 1257a4563) live in unrelated
files and are unaffected.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: revert: restore shell chrome, mode selector pill, and shared right-click menu

rawCommits.subject: feat(mode-selector): re-port per-mode tint onto restored animated indicator
rawCommits.body:
Previously 42ba4dc69 added per-mode border accents to the active
selection pill (success/green for Code, warning/amber for Plan,
info/cyan for Image, purple for 3D). That commit assumed the trim's
plain `.segmentActive { background; border }` shape, which the
preceding revert undid in favor of the absolutely-positioned animated
`.indicator` pill that slides between segments.

Re-port the per-mode accents onto the restored indicator instead:

- Tag the indicator span with `data-mode={selectedMode}` so CSS can
  match the active mode without React re-applying inline styles.
- Give `.indicator` a default `1px solid var(--color-border)` ring and
  add per-mode overrides via `.indicator[data-mode="…"]` that animate
  alongside the existing transform/width transition on
  `.indicatorReady`.

Also fix two adjacent issues uncovered while re-running the suite:

- `interface/src/test/setup.ts`: install a global no-op
  ResizeObserver. JSDOM doesn't ship one, so consumers of the
  animated mode pill (ChatInputBar, DesktopShell sidekick scrollbar,
  use-overflow-tabs, etc.) crashed at mount in tests that didn't
  install their own mock. The trim accidentally masked this by
  removing the only ResizeObserver caller in ModeSelector.
- `interface/src/components/UpdateBanner/UpdatePill.module.css`:
  tighten the pill's horizontal padding from `var(--space-3)` (12px)
  to `7px` so the compact pill sits cleanly in the titlebar actions
  next to the theme toggle.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: re-port per-mode tint onto restored animated indicator

rawCommits.subject: style(mode-selector): always tint selection pill with the accent color
rawCommits.body:
The pill now reads as a single, consistent accent regardless of mode
instead of switching hues per mode (success/warning/info/purple).

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: style(mode-selector): always tint selection pill with the accent color

rawCommits.subject: fix(process-canvas): darken light-mode dot grid so it's visible
rawCommits.body:
The light-mode --color-canvas-grid (#e5e7eb) sat right on top of the
near-white canvas background (#f9fafb), making the ReactFlow dot grid
effectively invisible. Bump it to #cbd5e1 so the grid reads cleanly
without competing with edges (--color-canvas-edge: #9ca3af).

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: darken light-mode dot grid so it's visible

rawCommits.subject: refactor(mode-selector): drive sliding pill from a CSS index variable
rawCommits.body:
Rewrite ModeSelector from the ground up so the indicator's left/right slide is driven entirely by CSS rather than DOM measurement. The segments grid uses equal 1fr columns and a single `--mode-index` custom property; the pill's transform is computed via calc(), and a CSS transition on transform produces the smooth animation. This drops useLayoutEffect, useState, ResizeObserver, the per-mode ref object, and the indicatorReady transition-suppression hack. The component remains plain HTML/CSS with no ZUI primitives, and its public API, ARIA semantics, keyboard navigation, and data-* hooks are unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: drive sliding pill from a CSS index variable

rawCommits.subject: fix(mode-selector): make pill actually slide between columns
rawCommits.body:
The previous transform reused `--mode-col` (which is defined as `calc((100% - ...) / count)`) inside the indicator's translateX. That looked symmetrical with the width declaration, but the `100%` token resolves against a different reference depending on the property: for `width` it's the containing block (the segments container, what we want), but for `transform: translateX` it's the element's own box. So the calc evaluated to the wrong distance and the pill effectively snapped instead of sliding to the new column. Use `100%` directly inside the transform — which on translateX is one column width by construction — and add `--mode-gap` once per index step to land on the exact left edge of column N. Width still resolves correctly via `--mode-col`.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: make pill actually slide between columns

rawCommits.subject: feat(agents-chats): redesign Chats tab as ChatGPT-style date-bucketed list
rawCommits.body:
Reshape the agent info Chats tab so sessions render as a single, flat,
reverse-chronological list bucketed by date (Today / Yesterday / Previous
7 Days / Previous 30 Days / Month Year) instead of grouped by project.
Drop the Explorer tree so child rows visually align with the bucket
header text. Replace the "S{n}" session prefix with the Haiku-generated
summary text alone (or "New chat" when none exists yet), and trigger the
existing /summarize endpoint as soon as a session has had its first
user+assistant turn so titles populate the way ChatGPT's do, instead of
waiting until the session is no longer active.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: redesign Chats tab as ChatGPT-style date-bucketed list

rawCommits.subject: fix(mode-selector): emit literal transform per render so the slide actually animates
rawCommits.body:
When the indicator's transform was written as `translateX(calc(var(--mode-index) * (100% + var(--mode-gap))))` and React only updated the `--mode-index` custom property, Chromium kept the transform's computed value as the same calc/var expression and treated it as unchanged — so the CSS transition on transform never fired and the pill snapped instantly to the new column. Move the transform off CSS variables: React now emits a fully-resolved `translateX(calc(N * 100% + N*gap px))` string per active mode. Each render produces a different computed transform value, which lets the transition interpolate between the two positions for the smooth left/right slide. The gap is mirrored as a `MODE_GAP_PX` constant in TS to stay in sync with `--mode-gap` in the CSS module. Tests updated to assert the exact transform string per mode.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: emit literal transform per render so the slide actually animates

rawCommits.subject: fix(aura3d): resolve eight 3D viewer + chat 3D mode issues
rawCommits.body:
- WebGLViewer: coalesce ResizeObserver into one rAF and render
  synchronously inside that frame so dragging the window edge no
  longer flashes a blank/stretched canvas; pin canvas size to 100%
  via CSS so setSize tracks the flex layout.
- Theme-aware viewer chrome: scene background and grid colors now
  switch with [data-theme] on <html>. Light mode uses a white
  background and a dark gray grid (instead of the unreadable blue
  on white). The container background uses --color-bg so any
  pre-paint frame reads correctly in both themes.
- Toggle buttons (Grid / Wireframe / Texture) drop the green accent
  and use the standard neutral selected styling that matches the
  segmented controls used elsewhere.
- Image -> 3D flow: selectImage and setActiveTab no longer
  auto-promote a previously-generated 3D model into
  current3DModel. The 3D tab now always offers the source image
  + Generate button, even when a model already exists; previously
  generated models are still reachable by clicking the 3D thumb in
  the sidekick or left-nav.
- Right-click delete: new deleteImage / deleteModel store
  actions wired to SidekickItemContextMenu for both the sidekick
  thumbnail grids (Images and 3D Models) and the main-panel image
  preview / 3D viewer surfaces. Deleting an image cascades to any
  linked 3D model.
- Larger main-panel images: dropped the 360px caps in
  ImagePreview, ImageGeneration and ModelGeneration so the preview
  fills the available area while a comfortable padding keeps it
  away from the prompt input.
- Chat 3D mode actually works: 3D mode in useAgentChatStream
  now bypasses the agent and calls generate3dStream directly
  with the pasted/uploaded image as a data: URL. The model
  picker shows a new Tripo v2 entry filtered through
  getModelsForMode("3d"). The backend Generate3dRequest
  accepts image_data (data URL) alongside the existing
  image_url, so chat 3D no longer needs a pre-existing artifact.
- STYLE_LOCK_SUFFIX is documented as AURA-3D-app only and is not
  appended on the chat-mode generation paths, so chat-mode
  prompts go to the provider unmodified.
rawCommits.cleanSubject: resolve eight 3D viewer + chat 3D mode issues

rawCommits.subject: feat(image-ux): shared Gallery + scroll-pin and accent fixes for image flows
rawCommits.body: - Adds a single shared `Gallery` overlay (`components/Gallery/`) with prev/next, ESC + backdrop dismiss, optional download, and a no-op fallback when no provider is mounted. Mounted once at the root in `main.tsx`. - Wires `useGallery().openGallery(...)` into MessageBubble user attachments, generated `ImageBlock` results, and the 3D sidekick thumbnails so every image surface in chat / project chat / 3D opens the same in-app viewer instead of scattered ad-hoc lightboxes or new-tab links. - New `useImageScrollPin` hook listens for descendant `` `load` events on the chat scroll container and re-pins to the bottom while the user is auto-following or inside the cold-load reveal window, fixing both "newly generated image scrolls past the bottom" and "opening a chat with images doesn't snap to the last message". - Tightens the 3D sidekick `.thumbSelected` border to use only `--color-accent` (with an accent-muted ring) so asset selection follows the active accent instead of a hardcoded green fallback. Tests: Gallery interaction suite, image-scroll-pin hook suite, and chat-ui-store image-mode init regression covering the persisted image-mode model load + sync paths. Co-authored-by: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: shared Gallery + scroll-pin and accent fixes for image flows

rawCommits.subject: refactor(sliding-pills): extract generic sliding-pill component, compose ModeSelector on top
rawCommits.body:
Pull the segmented-pill chrome and slide animation out of ModeSelector into a generic `SlidingPills` component under `interface/src/components/SlidingPills/` (TSX + module CSS + tests + index, per the project's React component layout rules). The component is controlled (`value` + `onChange`), strongly typed via a string-literal generic, exposes a `role=radiogroup` with `role=radio` segments, supports Left/Right (with wrap-around) and Home/End keyboard navigation, and handles disabled segments. The pill is sized and translated from the active button's measured `getBoundingClientRect`, so variable-width segments work; transitions on transform / width / height drive the smooth slide. ModeSelector is now a thin composition: it owns the row chrome (border, MODE label, padding, mobile breakpoints) and feeds the four agent modes to `SlidingPills` as `items`, keeping its public API (`selectedMode` / `onChange` / `className` / `hideLabel`) and `data-agent-surface` / `data-agent-mode` analytics hooks. Tests are split: positioning / keyboard / disabled-segment behavior live with `SlidingPills`, and `ModeSelector` tests focus on what the wrapper adds (radiogroup wiring, MODE label visibility, analytics surface).

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: extract generic sliding-pill component, compose ModeSelector on top

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat-3d): dispatch project-scoped 3D mode sends to Tripo instead of the LLM
rawCommits.body:
`useChatStream` (project agent chats) only branched on `image` generation
mode, so 3D-mode sends fell through to `api.sendEventStream` and ended up
as regular LLM turns. Mirrored the standalone-agent path: validate that
an image attachment is present, then call `generate3dStream` with a data
URL. Adds tests for the success path and the missing-image error path.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: dispatch project-scoped 3D mode sends to Tripo instead of the LLM

rawCommits.subject: fix(image-ux): re-pin chat after image-mode generation completes
rawCommits.body:
The `useImageScrollPin` hook used a capturing `load` listener that
relied on browser-specific propagation behaviour and was bypassed
once the user lost auto-follow mid-generation. As a result, after
sending an Image-mode prompt the viewport stayed at the top of the
new assistant bubble while the generated image grew the layout out
the bottom of the visible area.

- Switch the hook to a `ResizeObserver` on the scroll container's
  inner content wrapper. It catches every layout-shifting source
  (image decode, font load, dynamic tool-row expansion, gallery
  close, ...) without depending on `load` event propagation rules.
  The capturing `load` listener stays as belt-and-suspenders so
  immediate decode pins before the next layout flush.
- Bump the `imagePinUntil` window in `ChatPanel` whenever
  `isStreaming` toggles. The window now covers both the entire
  generation interval and the post-stream decode tail, so even if
  the user nudges auto-follow off during generation the final
  bubble still lands at the bottom.
- Extend the test suite to cover both the `ResizeObserver` path and
  the existing `load` path.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: re-pin chat after image-mode generation completes

rawCommits.subject: fix(theme): sync browser tab/PWA chrome to in-app ZUI theme
rawCommits.body:
The <meta name="theme-color"> tag in interface/index.html was keyed off
prefers-color-scheme media queries, so the browser's tab thumbnail and
URL bar tracked the OS preference instead of AURA's persisted ZUI theme
in localStorage["zui-theme"]. A user on OS=dark with AURA=light saw a
dark Chrome tab while the app rendered light.

Replace the dual media-keyed metas with a single <meta id="aura-theme-color">
tag, give the manifest <link> an id, and drive both from the resolved
theme. The pre-React inline boot script sets them on the first frame so
there's no flash, and a new BrowserChromeThemeBridge mounted under
<ThemeProvider> keeps them in sync on subsequent toggles. A sibling
manifest-light.webmanifest carries the light palette for fresh PWA
installs and installed-PWA contexts that re-read the manifest.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: sync browser tab/PWA chrome to in-app ZUI theme

rawCommits.subject: feat(aura3d): replace 3D Generate button with prompt input bar so the image stops resizing across tabs
rawCommits.body:
Image preview was visibly larger on the 3D Model tab because that tab
ended in a 76px button row while the Image tab ended in a tall
InputBarShell + model picker. Switching tabs jumped the image.

Generalize aura3d's PromptInput to take an optional `models` list and a
`requireText` flag, mirror the imagine prompt/model state with
`model3DPrompt` / `model3DModel` (defaulting to MODEL_3D_MODELS[0],
cleared on completion), and render the same input shell at the bottom
of ModelGeneration. Submitting forwards the trimmed prompt to
generate3dStream's existing optional `prompt` argument so the new text
field is functional, not cosmetic. Match `.viewerArea` padding to
`.previewArea` and drop the obsolete `.footer`/`.actionBar` rules so
both tabs render the source image at identical bounds.

Also updates the Image tab placeholder to clarify the input describes
a static reference image for the downstream 3D model.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: replace 3D Generate button with prompt input bar so the image stops resizing across tabs

rawCommits.subject: fix(aura3d): clicking a sidekick image only re-selects it instead of opening the gallery
rawCommits.body:
Sidekick thumbs were calling `selectImage` and `openGallery` on click,
which meant a single tap to swap the main-panel preview unavoidably
threw the user into the full-screen gallery. Drop the gallery launch
(and the now-unused `useGallery` / `galleryItems` plumbing) so left
click is just selection. Right-click still opens the delete menu, and
the gallery remains reachable from other entry points.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: clicking a sidekick image only re-selects it instead of opening the gallery

rawCommits.subject: fix(sliding-pills): make the indicator actually slide between segments
rawCommits.body:
- Drop the @media (prefers-reduced-motion) override on .indicator. The
  slide is the only visual feedback that selection moved, so disabling
  it under reduced-motion produced an unconditional snap instead of
  serving accessibility.
- Write transform / width / height imperatively on the indicator ref
  inside useLayoutEffect instead of going through useState. Holding
  the rect in state caused two commits per click (old rect, then new
  rect) before paint, which Chromium can collapse into a single
  style-change cycle so the transform delta never triggers the CSS
  transition. One imperative DOM write per click gives the browser a
  clean previous -> next transform between paints, which is what fires
  the slide.
- Tighten the duration from 220ms to 140ms so the motion reads as a
  snappy slide instead of a soft drift.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: make the indicator actually slide between segments

rawCommits.subject: feat(desktop-bg): per-theme backgrounds for light and dark mode
rawCommits.body:
The desktop background store now holds two BackgroundConfig slots
({ light, dark }) and BackgroundLayer renders whichever matches the
current ZUI resolvedTheme, so the wallpaper switches automatically when
the user toggles the theme. The right-click "Set Background..." modal
shows both Light Mode and Dark Mode sections stacked, each with its own
swatches, custom color picker, and image upload.

A legacy localStorage value (single { mode, color, imageDataUrl }) is
migrated by copying the existing config into both slots so users keep
the same wallpaper until they customize one explicitly.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: per-theme backgrounds for light and dark mode

rawCommits.subject: feat(chat-3d): allow text-to-3D when no source image is attached
rawCommits.body:
Tripo's 3D pipeline supports text-to-3D, but both chat-stream hooks and
the `/api/generate/3d/stream` handler hard-required an image and erred
out with "3D mode requires an attached or pasted image..." Relaxed both
sides so 3D mode can be driven by a prompt alone:

- `generate3dStream` accepts `null` / `undefined` source and omits
  `image_url` / `image_data` from the body in that case.
- `useChatStream` and `useAgentChatStream` only require a prompt OR an
  image (not both), and pass the user's actual typed text as the prompt
  rather than the synthesized "[N file(s)]" attachment label.
- The server handler now requires only that *one* of image_url /
  image_data / prompt is present, forwarding the empty image_url through
  to the upstream router for the text-to-3D path.

Updates the use-chat-stream tests: replaces the now-obsolete
"image required" case with positive coverage for text-only 3D and a
guard test for the no-prompt-no-image case via a non-image attachment.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: allow text-to-3D when no source image is attached

rawCommits.subject: refactor(chat-3d): model the no-image case as an explicit `none` variant
rawCommits.body:
Replace the nullish-source overload on `generate3dStream` with a third
discriminator on `Generate3dSource`: `{ kind: "none" }`. Callers now say
what they mean instead of leaning on `null` / `undefined` to imply the
text-to-3D path. The chat-stream hooks pass `{ kind: "none" }` when no
image is attached and the body still ships without `image_url` /
`image_data`.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: model the no-image case as an explicit `none` variant

rawCommits.subject: fix(desktop-bg): remove unused DEFAULTS constant
rawCommits.body:
Leftover from the per-theme background refactor; tsc --noUnusedLocals
was failing the build (TS6133).

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: remove unused DEFAULTS constant

rawCommits.subject: fix(theme): wire BrowserChromeThemeBridge into index.html and main
rawCommits.body:
Follow-up to 0fe61f9 ("fix(theme): sync browser tab/PWA chrome to in-app
ZUI theme"), which committed the new bridge component and the
manifest-light file but missed the three call-site modifications that
actually make the bridge run. Without these, the browser theme-color
meta and manifest link still ignored the in-app ZUI theme.

- index.html: collapse the dual prefers-color-scheme theme-color metas
  into one tag we own (id=aura-theme-color), give the manifest link
  id=aura-manifest, and have the inline boot script point both at the
  right palette from the persisted data-theme so the very first frame
  is correct.
- main.tsx: mount BrowserChromeThemeBridge under <ThemeProvider> so the
  meta + manifest stay in sync when the user toggles theme at runtime.
- pwa-mobile.spec.ts: assert exactly one theme-color meta now exists
  and that it carries our id, locking in the new shape.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: wire BrowserChromeThemeBridge into index.html and main

rawCommits.subject: fix(theme): alias --color-bg-subtle/-bg-elevated/-border-strong so light mode is light
rawCommits.body:
The in-app Browser app's tab strip painted as a black slab in light
mode (visible above the URL bar in the browser instance). Root cause:
BrowserInstanceTabs.module.css and BrowserErrorOverlay.module.css
reach for `var(--color-bg-subtle, #141414)` / `var(--color-bg-elevated,
#1a1a1a)` / `var(--color-border-strong, rgba(255,255,255,0.24))`, but
none of those tokens are defined anywhere in ZUI or our tokens.css —
so the dark-hex fallback fires in BOTH themes and the surface stays
dark when [data-theme=light] flips everything else white. The same
broken tokens leak into debug, agents, files, and modal CSS too, so
the fix is best applied centrally.

Add three theme-aware aliases next to the existing legacy-name
aliases (`--color-bg-secondary`, `--color-text`, `--color-bg-hover`)
that dereference into ZUI's per-theme `--color-surface`,
`--color-elevated`, and `--color-border` tokens. Every consumer now
flips with the active theme without touching any module CSS.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: alias --color-bg-subtle/-bg-elevated/-border-strong so light mode is light

rawCommits.subject: feat(aura3d): float viewer controls bottom-right and make 3D viewer full-bleed
rawCommits.body:
Move the grid/wireframe/texture toggles (and poly count) out of the
header strip above the canvas into a translucent overlay anchored to
the bottom-right of the viewer, and drop the viewerArea padding +
container border-radius so the 3D viewer fills the full middle section
flush with the panel edges below the tab bar.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: float viewer controls bottom-right and make 3D viewer full-bleed

rawCommits.subject: fix(aura3d): inset 3D source preview to match Image section framing
rawCommits.body:
Pad `.sourcePreview` so the source image shown in the 3D panel before
generation sits in the same inset frame as the image in the Image
section, instead of bleeding to the panel edges. The WebGL viewer
(`.viewerWrapper`) intentionally keeps no padding so the grid stays
full bleed once the 3D model is generated.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: inset 3D source preview to match Image section framing

rawCommits.subject: feat(aura3d): show pending thumbnail and clean loading panel during image generation
rawCommits.body:
Starting a new image generation now deselects the previously selected
image so the main preview renders its clean loading/spinner state
instead of overlaying the prior image, and a fresh selected placeholder
thumbnail is pinned at the front of the Images sidekick (promoting to
the streamed partial image once one arrives). `generateSourceImage` is
intentionally preserved so the 3D tab's source preview / Generate
button don't disappear mid-flight.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: show pending thumbnail and clean loading panel during image generation

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat-3d): require image attachment, surface inline upload hint
rawCommits.body:
Revert the optimistic text-to-3D path: aura-router only exposes
image-to-3D for Tripo today, so prompt-only requests bounced back as a
confusing 502 wrapping an upstream 422. Restore the image requirement on
both the server (`generate_3d_stream`) and the API surface
(`Generate3dSource` is back to a `url`/`data` union), and the chat
stream hooks now block 3D sends without an image.

Replace the previous hard error with a calmer UX: when 3D mode is
active and no image is attached, the desktop and mobile composers show
a persistent inline hint ("Upload or paste an image of a 3D object to
generate.") and the send button no longer dispatches the request — it
flashes the hint and keeps the input + attachments intact so the user
can drop in an image and retry without retyping. The stream-level
guard remains as defense-in-depth for paths that bypass the composer.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: require image attachment, surface inline upload hint

rawCommits.subject: feat(aura3d): show pending thumbnail and clean loading panel during 3D generation
rawCommits.body:
Mirrors the image-side behavior on the 3D Model tab. Clicking Generate
now:

- Drops `current3DModel` / `selectedModelId` so the main viewer
  renders a clean spinner + rotating progress message instead of
  continuing to show the source image as if nothing happened.
- Pins a fresh selected pending placeholder at the top of the Models
  sidekick, using the source image as its dim base with a centered
  spinner overlay so the user has a concrete "selected" target the
  moment they click Generate.

`generateSourceImage` is intentionally preserved while in flight: it
powers the pending thumb's base image and is still needed by the
streaming generation request itself.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: show pending thumbnail and clean loading panel during 3D generation

rawCommits.subject: feat(aura3d): capture and persist 3D model snapshots as sidekick thumbnails
rawCommits.body:
When the user opens a generated 3D model in the AURA 3D viewer, the
freshly-rendered scene is snapped to a PNG and POSTed to a new
/api/artifacts/:id/thumbnail endpoint that writes it to
data_dir/workspaces/<project>/.thumbnails/<artifact_id>.png. The
sidekick "3D Models" tile then renders the actual model preview
instead of the cube placeholder, with an onError fallback chain to
the source image and finally the cube icon when no PNG exists yet.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: capture and persist 3D model snapshots as sidekick thumbnails

rawCommits.subject: fix(image-mode): persist image-mode generations into chat history so they survive reload
rawCommits.body:
Image-mode (slash-command / mode picker) generations were lost on hard
reload because `POST /api/generate/image/stream` only opened a synthetic
harness session and streamed events back; the UI's `generate_image`
tool turn was synthesized client-side from `GenerationCompleted` and
lived only in the in-memory zustand stream store.

Now the request carries the chat scope (`agentId` for standalone agent
chat, `projectId` + `agentInstanceId` for project chat), the server
resolves the same `ChatPersistCtx` the regular chat route uses, and a
sibling task spawned alongside the SSE adapter writes a `user_message`
+ synthetic `assistant_message_end` row pair. The assistant row's
`content_blocks` mirror what the chat-agent persists when its LLM
calls `generate_image` itself, so cold reload renders the image
through the existing `ImageBlock` path with no UI changes.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: persist image-mode generations into chat history so they survive reload

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat-3d): inline 3D image hint inside input row, center and space inputRowEnd
rawCommits.body:
Move the "Add an image to generate." chip from the stacked containerTop
slot into the absolutely-positioned inputRowEnd slot so toggling 3D
mode no longer changes the input bar's height. Shorten the label and
add a `.threeDHintInline` modifier that drops the stacked-layout outer
margin.

Also adjust the shared InputBarShell `.inputRowEnd` wrapper:
- Vertically center via top: 50% + translateY(-50%) instead of
  bottom: 5px so chips sit on the input's midline (was bottom-heavy).
- Bump right offset (42 -> 52 desktop, 50 -> 58 mobile) so inline
  chips have breathing room from the send button's hit area.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: inline 3D image hint inside input row, center and space inputRowEnd

rawCommits.subject: fix(generation): raise SSE body cap to 25 MiB for image / 3D streams
rawCommits.body:
Chat 3D mode inlines pasted images as base64 data URLs in the request
body, which overruns axum's 2 MiB default and surfaces as
`SSE request failed (413): Failed to buffer the request body: length
limit exceeded`. Apply a `DefaultBodyLimit` layer to both generation
routes so realistic screenshots / photos go through.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: raise SSE body cap to 25 MiB for image / 3D streams

rawCommits.subject: fix(aura3d): make 3D sidekick thumbnails actually render
rawCommits.body: The captured-PNG thumbnail feature shipped in cafe3915a was broken in three compounding ways: the upload never fired in practice and even if it had, couldn't authenticate against the GET endpoint. - artifactToModel synthesised /api/artifacts/:id/thumbnail for every loaded model, which made currentModelHasThumbnail truthy and short-circuited the upload before any PNG was written. Drop the guard; WebGLViewer's capturedUrlRef already dedupes per mount. - complete3DGeneration was called without artifactId, so the upload bailed on `if (!model?.artifactId) return` for fresh generations until a project reload. Forward event.content.artifactId from the GenerationCompleted SSE event. - The thumbnail GET route lives under protected_api_router. A bare request carries no Authorization header, so the server returned 401 and tiles fell through to the cube placeholder. Wrap the synthetic + post-upload URLs with ?token= (the same fallback the backend already accepts for SSE / WebSockets). Co-authored-by: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: make 3D sidekick thumbnails actually render

rawCommits.subject: feat(aura3d): confirm right-click delete for images and 3D models
rawCommits.body:
Route every Aura3D context-menu Delete (main image preview, 3D viewer,
source-image preview, and sidekick gallery thumbs for both Images and
Models) through the standard `ModalConfirm` from `@cypher-asi/zui`,
matching how destructive actions are confirmed elsewhere in the app
(e.g. `AutomationBar`).

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: confirm right-click delete for images and 3D models

rawCommits.subject: fix(generation): cap inlined 3D source images at 8 MiB with clear 413
rawCommits.body:
Even after the 25 MiB route-level body cap, oversized base64 `data:`
URLs clear aura-os-server but stall in the harness / aura-router
pipeline downstream — surfacing as the SSE watchdog's generic
"Image generation did not start. Try again in a moment." 120 s later,
which is opaque from the user's perspective.

Reject `data:` URLs above 8 MiB up front in `generate_3d_stream`
with a `413 payload_too_large` and a message that names the size
and the cap. Hosted `https://` URLs are tiny strings and skip the
check, so the AURA 3D app's flow is unaffected. Adds an
`ApiError::payload_too_large` constructor in passing.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: cap inlined 3D source images at 8 MiB with clear 413

rawCommits.subject: Revert "fix(generation): raise SSE body cap to 25 MiB for image / 3D streams"
rawCommits.body: This reverts commit 2a201a15da34b56f925a3d739e53b1f129d304cc.
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Revert "fix(generation): raise SSE body cap to 25 MiB for image / 3D streams"

rawCommits.subject: chore(modes): hide 3D mode from mode selector for now
rawCommits.body:
Comment out "3d" in AGENT_MODE_ORDER so the selector renders only
Code / Plan / Image. The AgentMode type, descriptor, and downstream
3D generation pipeline are left intact so re-enabling is a one-line
revert. Tests updated to assert 3D mode is absent.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: hide 3D mode from mode selector for now

rawCommits.subject: perf(interface): drop ineffective dynamic import of aura3d-store
rawCommits.body:
aura3d-store is statically imported by Aura3DMainPanel, Aura3DNav,
Aura3DSidekickPanel, Aura3DSidekickTaskbar, ImageGeneration,
ModelGeneration, PromptInput, and useOnboardingTaskWatcher, so the
dynamic imports in AppShell.resetCaptureAppSpecificState and
capture-bridge.applyAuraCaptureSeedPlan never moved the module into
its own chunk and only added async indirection. Vite was already
warning about this with [INEFFECTIVE_DYNAMIC_IMPORT]. Switch both
sites to the static import that already exists transitively.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: perf(interface): drop ineffective dynamic import of aura3d-store

rawCommits.subject: chore(server): remove duplicate `use super::*` in agent-instances tests
rawCommits.body:
Two identical `use super::*` statements topped
`handlers::agents::instances::tests`. The second was flagged as
`unused_imports` by rustc and contributed no symbols beyond the
first. Drop it.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: remove duplicate `use super::*` in agent-instances tests

rawCommits.subject: fix(server): keep chat_persist_unavailable (424) precedence over Tier-1 preflight
rawCommits.body:
The Tier-1 session-identity preflight added in 31e24487c runs inside
`open_harness_chat_stream` before `require_persist_ctx`. Because
`SessionConfig.aura_session_id` is sourced from `persist_ctx`, a None
`persist_ctx` was being caught by the preflight as a generic
`missing_aura_session_id` (HTTP 422) instead of the documented,
contract-stable `chat_persist_unavailable` (HTTP 424) that
`send_to_agent` consumers parse and act on. The preflight only
matters if we are about to dispatch to the harness, which we never
do without a persist context, so the persistence precondition is
the more fundamental check and must run first.

Reorder the two checks and add a comment explaining the invariant
so this regression doesn't recur.

Also update the `chat_events_test::streaming` regression test that
guards this contract:

* Inject a small inline billing mock so the test passes the now-
  uniform `require_credits_for_auth_source` guard introduced in
  8b9fbd910 ("route all model traffic through the Aura proxy"),
  which removed the per-`auth_source` bypass the test previously
  relied on.
* Refresh the stale `local`-bypass comment to reflect the new
  uniform credits policy.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: keep chat_persist_unavailable (424) precedence over Tier-1 preflight

rawCommits.subject: feat(aura3d): bind deleteModel selector in ModelGeneration
rawCommits.body:
The right-click delete confirm flow now delegates to the store's
`deleteModel` action when the pending delete targets a 3D model
(it was already wired up for the image case via `deleteImage`).
Bind the selector so the existing `pendingDelete.id` branch can
call it and add it to the effect's deps array.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: bind deleteModel selector in ModelGeneration

rawCommits.subject: fix(interface): prevent horizontal scroll in Settings modal sidebar
rawCommits.body:
Clip horizontal overflow on the OrgSettingsPanel left nav and ellipsize
long team names in the nav header so the sidebar fits the fixed 220px
column without a stray bottom scrollbar.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: prevent horizontal scroll in Settings modal sidebar

rawCommits.subject: feat(interface): compact background modal with Color/Image type toggle
rawCommits.body:
Each theme section now starts with a small Color | Image toggle that
gates the controls below, so only the relevant view renders. Removes
the dedicated default-reset swatch in favor of pre-selected white
(light) / black (dark) defaults, which are also added to the preset
swatch grid.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: compact background modal with Color/Image type toggle

rawCommits.subject: fix(interface): stabilize Settings > Updates row and align "Last checked" with description
rawCommits.body:
- Shorten the team Updates row description to "Check and install new versions."
- Move "Last checked: / <date>" from below the button to under the description in the left rowInfo column, sharing the rowDescription styling so the two lines align with the description text.
- Render an equivalent "Last checked" dt/dd row in the App > About panel for consistency.
- Stop the row from jerking when "Check for updates" is clicked: the inline checking state no longer wipes the actions slot and replaces the message with a spinner; instead the button itself shows the spinner+"Checking..." while the row layout stays put.
- Expose `formatLastChecked` and add a `showLastChecked` prop on `UpdateControl` so consumers can render the timestamp themselves.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: stabilize Settings > Updates row and align "Last checked" with description

rawCommits.subject: fix(interface): polish Settings panel rewards/billing/appearance UX
rawCommits.body:
Cache the user invite code in a zustand+localStorage store so opening Rewards
no longer flashes a loading state on every visit, and reset it on logout. Move
subscription status into the billing store and pre-warm it when the Settings
panel opens, plus reserve layout space for the optional billing-period row, so
navigating into Billing (or Z Credit History) no longer jerks while the
subscription resolves.

Tighten the Rewards copy (clearer reward descriptions, "Upgrade for more"),
add an Upgrade CTA that opens the tier modal, rename the transaction row's
"bal:" label to "Balance:", and hardcode the shareable invite link to
https://aura.ai so links generated from native or local hosts still work for
recipients.

In the Settings sidebar, color the Logout button to match the Navigator items
instead of fading it out. In the Appearance section, force the Light/Dark/
System theme buttons onto a single row and surface "Custom colors" above
"Theme presets" since users hit it more often.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: polish Settings panel rewards/billing/appearance UX

rawCommits.subject: fix(interface): add blank line between Updates description and "Last checked"
rawCommits.body:
Insert an empty `rowDescription` span (nbsp) between "Check and install new versions." and "Last checked:" so the timestamp visually separates from the description.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: add blank line between Updates description and "Last checked"

rawCommits.subject: fix(interface): clarify "Earn More" upgrade copy
rawCommits.body:
Reword the description in the team rewards Earn More row to call out that upgrading boosts the monthly Z credit allowance and the daily bonus.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: clarify "Earn More" upgrade copy

rawCommits.subject: feat(interface): hover-only overlay scrollbar in Settings left nav
rawCommits.body:
Match the chat sidebar / LeftMenu UX so the Settings nav scrolls
independently with a thin custom thumb that fades in on hover. Aligns
the shared OverlayScrollbar component to the LeftMenu design tokens
(--color-overlay-focus / --color-scrollbar-thumb-hover) so any future
reuse is visually consistent.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: hover-only overlay scrollbar in Settings left nav

rawCommits.subject: fix(agents): add view=slim to GET /api/agents to stop list_agents tool bloat
rawCommits.body:
The harness-native `list_agents` LLM tool currently calls `GET /api/agents`
and forwards the full `Vec<Agent>` (icons, system_prompt, personality,
permissions, ...) into the tool result. A single `Agent` JSON entry runs
~15-50KB because of the base64 WebP icon, so even a small fleet overflows
the harness's 8000-char per-tool-result cap mid-record before the model
can read agent names past the first one or two -- producing the observed
"every list_agents call gets truncated before I reach the name fields".

Add an opt-in `view=slim` query knob that returns `Vec<AgentSummary>`
with exactly `{agent_id, name, role}` instead. Default stays `full` so
the UI sidebar / AgentSelectorModal / editor keep their full payload.
The slim path also skips the icon shadow merge, runtime-config apply,
permissions reconciliation, and background shadow flush since none of
that affects the slim shape and the tool path is hot.

Pin the wire shape with a unit test asserting the JSON keys are exactly
`{agent_id, name, role}` and that none of the heavy fields can sneak
back in. Field creep here is what re-bloats the tool result, so this
catches the regression cheaper than another round of harness-side
truncation patches.

Update the chat-history `TOOL_BLOB_MAX_BYTES` comment and the
`tool_dedupe` cross-agent note so the next person debugging a bloated
context finds the slim view immediately. The matching aura-harness
change (`AuraServerAgentHook::list_agents` appending `view=slim`) ships
in the sibling repo so the LLM sees the slim payload immediately.

Made-with: Cursor
Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: add view=slim to GET /api/agents to stop list_agents tool bloat

rawCommits.subject: fix(interface): stop CEO first-chat from streaming twice on rapid double-send
rawCommits.body:
The synchronous latch in `sendMessage` for both `useAgentChatStream` and
`useChatStream` covers the gap between invocation and the moment
`setIsStreaming(true)` propagates through Zustand. The previous
`getIsStreaming(core.key)` guard was racy when two calls landed in the
same microtask (double-click Send / Enter twice / queue-dequeue replay
reaching the hook before the first send had returned): both reads saw
`false`, both writes proceeded, and two parallel POSTs to the chat
stream endpoint fired.

That race surfaced especially loudly on the very first chat with the
CEO, where `lazy_repair_home_project_binding` makes
`send_agent_event_stream` hold the HTTP request for several seconds
before returning the SSE response — long enough that an impatient user
re-clicks and the second click squeezes through the gap.

A regression test pins the contract: a second `sendMessage` issued in
the same microtask while the first is mid-await must short-circuit and
must not produce a second user message.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: stop CEO first-chat from streaming twice on rapid double-send

rawCommits.subject: chore(chat): add gated tracer for CEO chat user-message flap diagnosis
rawCommits.body:
Multiple users have reported a CEO-chat regression where a just-sent
user message visibly flickers / is briefly overwritten before settling.
Recent commit history (`stop transcript flash on history flap mid-turn`,
`stop CEO transcript blink`, `stop stream history blink regression`,
`pin active history key in LRU to stop CEO chat blink`) shows this
family of races has been patched piecemeal — the comments at lines
156-166 of `use-conversation-snapshot.ts` literally describe a previous
flavor as "user prompt remains, all assistant content gone right when
the turn finishes".

Before guessing at another merge tweak, capture the actual event
timeline. Add a behind-flag tracer that's strictly opt-in:

  localStorage.setItem("aura.debug.chatMerge", "1") and reload

When enabled, the tracer logs every recompute branch in
`combineStoredAndStreamMessages` plus every stream-store mutation in
`useChatHistorySync` (post-stream forced refetch, two WS-triggered
refetches, two `resetEvents(...)` paths). The flag is read once at
module load so disabled-path cost is a single boolean read; no work
happens on the hot render path otherwise.

Lining the `[aura.chatMerge]` snapshot logs up against the matching
history-sync logs pinpoints which race is firing — stale post-stream
history clobber, WS refetch landing inside the merge's empty-frame
window, message-store thread reset, or `lastNonEmptyRef` cache miss —
without speculating across two stores and three subscriptions.

Phase 2 patch ships once we have a captured trace from a real repro;
either way this tracer can stay (DEV-gated) afterwards because the
recurrence pattern here strongly suggests the next regression is
already coming.

Made-with: Cursor
Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: add gated tracer for CEO chat user-message flap diagnosis

rawCommits.subject: feat(zui): add fullWidth prop to Button and use it in BackgroundModal toggle
rawCommits.body:
Adds a `fullWidth` prop on `Button` that switches it to `display: flex`,
`width: 100%`, `min-width: 0` and `flex: 1 1 0`, so a row of buttons in
a flex container shares the available horizontal space equally and the
default 200px `--control-width-default` floor stops constraining narrow
layouts.

Uses the new prop on the Color | Image toggle in the desktop background
modal so each button takes half the row and the pair is visually
centered.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: add fullWidth prop to Button and use it in BackgroundModal toggle

rawCommits.subject: feat(interface): hover-only overlay scrollbar in OrgSettingsPanel
rawCommits.body:
The Settings modal still showed the OS native scrollbar on both the
left nav and the right content area. Wrap each panel with the shared
OverlayScrollbar component (the same pattern used by chat sidebar /
LeftMenu) so the thumb fades in only on mouseover and disappears when
not needed. Native scrollbars are hidden on the inner scroll wrappers
via scrollbar-width:none and ::-webkit-scrollbar { display:none }.

Padding moves from the outer panels to the inner scroll wrappers so
content still has the same insets while the overlay thumb sits flush
against the panel edge.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: hover-only overlay scrollbar in OrgSettingsPanel

rawCommits.subject: fix(zui): strengthen Button .fullWidth specificity to override base min-width
rawCommits.body:
Uses the compound selector `.button.fullWidth` (specificity 0,2,0)
instead of `.fullWidth` (0,1,0) so the rule reliably beats the base
`.button` `min-width: var(--control-width-default)` regardless of
source-order assumptions or downstream override rules. Also makes
`flex-basis` explicit (`0%`) for older browsers that mishandle the
unitless `0` form.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: strengthen Button .fullWidth specificity to override base min-width

rawCommits.subject: fix(marketplace): unblock loading and surface hireable agents end-to-end
rawCommits.body:
The Marketplace app was stuck on "Loading marketplace..." and, once
unstuck, returned an empty list even after the user marked their own
agents hireable. Two compounding bugs:

1. Front-end refresh dedupe collided with the initial spinner flag.
   `useMarketplaceStore` started with `loading: true` to avoid an
   empty-state flash, but `refresh()` short-circuited whenever
   `loading` was true, so the very first call from
   `MarketplaceMainPanel` no-oped and the API was never hit. Decouple
   dedupe from the spinner: track an `inflight: Promise | null` field
   and dedupe on it; keep `loading: true` purely as the UI flag.

2. Back-end only queried the cross-user marketplace view. The handler
   called the new `GET /api/agents?listing_status=hireable&...` (per
   the Phase 3 migration doc), but if aura-network excludes the
   caller's own listings from that view, the user can never see the
   agents they just marked hireable. Union three sources by
   `agent_id`: marketplace > caller-scoped `client.list_agents` >
   local shadows. Marketplace wins on duplicates so its
   server-computed stats stay authoritative.

Also:

- Add `serde(alias = "listing_status")` and `alias = "revenue_usd"`
  to `NetworkAgent` so snake_case responses don't silently decode to
  `None` and get dropped by the hireable filter. The struct uses
  `rename_all = "camelCase"`, but the migration doc example and some
  upstream emitters use snake_case.
- Add `tracing::info!` source counts in `load_hireable_agents` and
  `tracing::debug!` the marketplace URL so we can tell which source
  surfaced a row when debugging future empty-marketplace reports.
- Tests: vitest 16/16 green; new dedup-by-id and distinct-id unit
  tests for the union helper; existing marketplace handler and
  network unit tests still green.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: unblock loading and surface hireable agents end-to-end

rawCommits.subject: refactor(zui): drop Button width baseline so it sizes to content or fullWidth
rawCommits.body:
Removes `min-width: var(--control-width-default)` from the base
`.button` rule. Buttons no longer carry a 200px floor; they size to
their content by default and can opt into filling via the existing
`fullWidth` prop. The token stays in `tokens.css` for `Input`, and
the design guide is updated to reflect the new, input-only role.

Applies `fullWidth` to the Light / Dark / System theme buttons in the
Appearance settings row so they share the row equally now that the
implicit floor is gone, mirroring the desktop background modal toggle.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: drop Button width baseline so it sizes to content or fullWidth

rawCommits.subject: fix(server): raise body limit on attachment-bearing routes
rawCommits.body:
Image-mode generation (and the chat / 3D streams that share the same
inlined-base64 attachment shape) ride Axum 0.7's default 2 MiB body
cap. Pasting a normal screenshot inflates past that almost immediately,
the connection closes mid-upload, and the browser surfaces it as
`TypeError: Failed to fetch` instead of a parsable 413.

Add a shared 16 MiB ATTACHMENT_REQUEST_MAX_BYTES (covers the chat
input bar's 10 MB total cap with base64 + JSON overhead) and apply it
per-route to:
- POST /api/generate/image/stream
- POST /api/generate/3d/stream
- POST /api/agents/:agent_id/events/stream
- POST /api/projects/:project_id/agents/:agent_instance_id/events/stream

Other routes keep the tighter default.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: raise body limit on attachment-bearing routes

rawCommits.subject: fix(left-menu): hide FolderSection parent chevrons until hover
rawCommits.body:
Bring Feedback (and other FolderSection consumers - Marketplace,
Integrations) in line with the project-tree pattern in LeftMenuTree:
parent chevrons are right-aligned, hidden by default, and fade in on
row hover/focus instead of being permanently visible.

Match LeftMenuTree's .projectChevronWrap exactly (color, opacity,
transition) so all left-menu parents share the same chevron behavior.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: hide FolderSection parent chevrons until hover

rawCommits.subject: refactor(chat): render attached images as a strip above the bubble
rawCommits.body:
User-pasted images now render in their own thumbnail strip above the
dark text bubble (sibling of `.bubble`, inside `.message`) instead of
sharing the bubble with the message text. Image-only messages skip
the bubble entirely so the strip stands alone.

Splits `contentBlocks` into image vs non-image groups while preserving
original indices so gallery `initialId` lookups stay 1:1. Resizes
`.messageImageWrapper` from a 480px-wide 16:9 panel to a 96px (104px
on mobile) square thumbnail with `object-fit: cover`, matching the
target preview row layout.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: render attached images as a strip above the bubble

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): retry live history refresh after delivery
rawCommits.body:
Add a settled follow-up refetch for matched chat delivery events so target agent panels recover when the immediate history read races storage visibility.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: retry live history refresh after delivery

rawCommits.subject: fix(server): stream image generation through router
rawCommits.body:
Route image-mode SSE directly to the router so startup failures surface promptly, and avoid persisting inline base64 reference images that exceed storage limits.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: stream image generation through router

rawCommits.subject: fix(server): avoid direct image router upload
rawCommits.body:
Restore image-mode streaming through the harness so large inline image inputs do not hit the router HTTP body buffer introduced by the previous change.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: avoid direct image router upload

rawCommits.subject: fix(image): keep pasted image generation responsive
rawCommits.body:
Stream image mode directly from the router, compress pasted image inputs before upload, and persist storage-safe references so history refreshes keep the thumbnail visible.

rawCommits.subject: fix(marketplace): polish talent card layout
rawCommits.body:
Make marketplace cards show taller cover art and avoid repeating role text when the description duplicates it.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: polish talent card layout

rawCommits.body: Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

rawCommits.body:
Adding --workspaces=false --include-workspace-root prevents the parent

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

rawCommits.subject: fix(feedback): float header above scroll area
rawCommits.body:
Make the feed header absolutely positioned so it overlays the scroll

rendered.title: AURA 3D, image flows, and Settings polish
rendered.intro: A heavy day for AURA's creative surfaces: chat 3D mode landed (and was carefully walked back to image-only), image generations now persist across reloads, the desktop wallpaper splits per theme, and the Settings panel got a wide pass for layout stability and overlay scrollbars. A late stretch hardened image upload paths through the server router and tightened the marketplace end-to-end.

entries.title: More accurate Mixpanel signals for DAU and checkout
entries.summary: Analytics events were realigned so DAU and purchase funnels reflect actual user behavior.

items.text: Added a session_active event from AppShell for accurate daily-active-user tracking.
Renamed purchase and subscription events in the Buy Credits and Tier modals to reflect checkout intent rather than completion, so funnel analytics no longer overcount conversions.

entries.title: AURA 3D viewer overhaul and chat 3D mode lifecycle
entries.summary: A long arc that restored the polished shell chrome, rebuilt the mode-selector pill animation, brought chat 3D mode online via Tripo, then walked it back to image-required after upstream limits surfaced — alongside a shared Gallery, per-theme desktop wallpapers, and Settings polish.

items.text: Reverted a regressing UI trim and restored the desktop titlebar's compact UpdatePill, the BottomTaskbar right-click menu, the inline CommandChips slot, and the AgentEditorForm Name/Role row.

Rebuilt the mode-selector indicator from the ground up — first as a CSS-variable slide, then with literal per-render transforms after Chromium collapsed the variable updates — and finally extracted a generic SlidingPills primitive (radiogroup, keyboard nav, variable-width segments) that ModeSelector composes on top.

Overhauled the AURA 3D app: theme-aware viewer chrome, coalesced ResizeObserver renders to stop blank frames on resize, full-bleed viewer with floating bottom-right toggles, larger main-panel images, right-click delete with confirm, pending thumbnails during generation, and captured PNG snapshots persisted as sidekick thumbnails via a new /api/artifacts/:id/thumbnail endpoint.

Brought chat 3D mode online by routing 3D sends through Tripo with pasted image data URLs in both standalone and project chats, briefly opened a text-to-3D path, then reverted to image-required when aura-router's upstream stayed image-only — replacing the hard error with an inline 'Add an image to generate.' hint and ultimately hiding 3D from the mode selector for now.

Shipped a single shared in-app Gallery overlay (prev/next, ESC, download) used by chat attachments, generated ImageBlock results, and 3D sidekick thumbs, plus a useImageScrollPin hook that re-pins the chat to the bottom while images decode mid-generation.

Image-mode generations now persist into chat history: the request carries chat scope and the server writes a user_message + assistant_message_end pair so generated images survive a hard reload through the existing ImageBlock path.

Hardened theme handling: browser tab and PWA chrome now sync to the in-app ZUI theme via a BrowserChromeThemeBridge and a sibling light manifest, the in-app Browser tab strip stops painting black in light mode, and the ReactFlow canvas dot grid is visible against the light background.

Added per-theme desktop wallpapers — the background store now holds separate light/dark slots and the modal exposes a Color/Image toggle with sensible white/black defaults; legacy single-config users are migrated into both slots.

Redesigned the agent info Chats tab as a flat, ChatGPT-style date-bucketed list (Today / Yesterday / Previous 7 Days / ...) with Haiku-summarized titles populated as soon as a session has its first turn.

Settings panel polish: stabilized the Updates row layout so 'Check for updates' no longer jerks, aligned 'Last checked' under the description, added a hover-only overlay scrollbar to the left nav, prewarmed billing subscription state and cached the invite code so Rewards/Billing stop flashing loading on every open, and forced Light/Dark/System onto a single row.

Server reliability for large attachments: raised the SSE body cap to 25 MiB for image/3D streams, added an 8 MiB cap on inline 3D data URLs with a clear 413, then reverted the 25 MiB bump after follow-up routing changes; chat-persist precedence (424) was kept ahead of the Tier-1 preflight so send_to_agent consumers see the contract-stable error.

entries.title: list_agents tool slimming and CEO double-send fix
entries.summary: Backend and chat-stream fixes targeting two specific user-visible failure modes — truncated agent listings inside the LLM tool path, and a duplicated first message on the CEO chat.

items.text: Added an opt-in view=slim parameter to GET /api/agents that returns just {agent_id, name, role}, so the harness-native list_agents tool no longer overflows its 8000-char per-result cap on icon-heavy agent fleets.

Fixed a race where rapid double-sends on the very first CEO chat could fire two parallel POSTs to the chat stream endpoint; sendMessage now uses a synchronous latch so the second invocation in the same microtask short-circuits.
Added a behind-flag chat-merge tracer (localStorage aura.debug.chatMerge=1) to capture the event timeline behind reported CEO-chat user-message flap reports without speculating across stores.

entries.title: Marketplace unblocked and ZUI Button width rework
entries.summary: The Marketplace finally surfaces hireable agents end-to-end, ZUI buttons stop carrying an implicit 200px floor, and the Settings modal gains overlay scrollbars and an attached-image strip in chat bubbles.

items.text: Unblocked the Marketplace: separated the loading flag from inflight dedupe so the first refresh actually hits the API, and unioned marketplace listings with caller-scoped agents and local shadows so users can see agents they marked hireable themselves.

Reworked ZUI Button sizing: dropped the 200px min-width baseline so buttons size to content by default, added a fullWidth prop with stronger specificity, and applied it to the Color/Image background toggle and the Light/Dark/System theme row.
Added hover-only overlay scrollbars to the OrgSettingsPanel left nav and content area, hiding the OS-native scrollbars to match chat sidebar / LeftMenu UX.
Raised the body limit on attachment-bearing routes to 16 MiB (image/3D streams and both chat event endpoints), so a normal pasted screenshot no longer surfaces as 'TypeError: Failed to fetch'.

Refined chat UI: pasted images now render as a 96px thumbnail strip above the bubble (image-only messages skip the bubble entirely), and FolderSection parent chevrons hide until row hover to match the project tree.

entries.title: Settled refetch for chat history after delivery
entries.summary: Closes a small race where the immediate history read after a delivery event could miss storage visibility.

items.text: Added a settled follow-up refetch for matched chat delivery events so target agent panels recover when the immediate history read races storage visibility.

entries.title: Image upload routing, marketplace hiring, and feedback header
entries.summary: An end-of-day stretch reworked image-mode streaming through the router with pasted-image compression, fixed marketplace hiring + task counts, and polished the feedback feed and talent cards.

items.text: Reworked image-mode streaming: first routed it directly through aura-router for prompt failure surfacing, then reverted when large inline inputs hit the router's body buffer, then landed a final shape that streams through the router with pasted-image compression on upload and storage-safe persisted references so reloads keep the thumbnail visible.
Marketplace flow now actually attaches hired agents and shows task counts on talent cards, with cleaner cover-art-heavy layout that avoids repeating role text when the description duplicates it.
Floated the Feedback feed header above the scroll area with matching top padding so content no longer hides behind it.
Scoped the ZUI postinstall to the vendor workspace so the parent install no longer recurses back into the interface package.

highlights: Chat 3D mode shipped via Tripo, then scoped to image-required
Image-mode generations now persist across reload
Per-theme desktop wallpapers with light/dark slots
Shared in-app Gallery for every image surface
Marketplace loading + hire flow fixed end-to-end
Settings panel stabilized with overlay scrollbars and prewarmed billing
